claim_boundary: TICKET112 proves an exact finite Farey-cell Abel identity and a no-go theorem for independent endpoint triangles on the audited family. The inherited endpoint-saving inequality only survives finite replay. It proves none of the four conjectures and certifies no conjecture counterexample.

exact_farey_abel_identity.cells: The fixed Q=32 minor mask has 162 connected Farey cells on every audited FFT grid.
exact_farey_abel_identity.identity: Within each cell, sum z_k p_k = Z_b p_b + sum_{k<b} Z_k(p_k-p_{k+1}), with z_k the Vaughan Type II cross-spectrum and p_k the shift-two phase.
exact_farey_abel_identity.interpretation: The first term is cross-cell endpoint mass; the second is controlled by the smooth within-cell phase variation.

endpoint_triangle_no_go.tested_bound: Take absolute values of every cell endpoint and every within-cell Abel variation term independently.
endpoint_triangle_no_go.finite_result: This improves sharply over the singleton-bin envelope but still leaves a negative lower bound on every audited horizon through 2M.
endpoint_triangle_no_go.holdout_reduction: At 2M the envelope falls from 7.596M to 1.280M, while the resulting total lower bound remains -351.1K.
endpoint_triangle_no_go.loss_location: At 2M, 96.05% of the Abel envelope is endpoint absolute mass; within-cell variation is only 3.95%.
endpoint_triangle_no_go.consequence: The next theorem should control cancellation among Farey-cell endpoint sums instead of further refining energy bins or smooth phase variation.
endpoint_triangle_no_go.scope: This refutes the endpoint-triangle version of the finite Farey-Abel route, not phase-aware large-sieve or circle-method estimates.

inherited_endpoint_candidate.selection_boundary: Q=32 and exponent 1/6 are inherited unchanged from TICKET111; no TICKET112 row was used to tune them.
inherited_endpoint_candidate.variation_treatment: The within-cell variation remains bounded by its full absolute Abel envelope.
inherited_endpoint_candidate.proof_gap: The observed endpoint cancellation must be derived uniformly from arithmetic structure. A finite endpoint table cannot establish that theorem or exclude adversarial future scales.

discarded_routes: Return to singleton-bin phase-blind Cauchy after the Farey-Abel reduction.
Take absolute values of all 162 Farey endpoints independently and call the reduction complete.
Infer endpoint power saving from one inherited holdout.
Use the small within-cell variation term as if it controlled cross-cell endpoint signs.
Transfer ternary Goldbach Farey estimates without matching the binary shifted-prime cross-spectrum.

literature_boundary.established_background: Farey rational dissection, Vaughan identity, smoothing, Abel summation, and weighted large-sieve estimates are established.
literature_boundary.project_specific_result: The exact endpoint-versus-variation audit for this fixed binary shift-two cross-spectrum and the no-go certificate for independent endpoint triangles.

twin_farey_endpoint_abel_audit.proof_boundary: TICKET112 proves an exact finite Farey-cell Abel identity and a no-go theorem for independent endpoint triangles on the audited family. The inherited endpoint-saving inequality only survives finite replay. It proves none of the four conjectures and certifies no conjecture counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: exact Abel reduction plus endpoint-triangle falsification and inherited holdout
attempts.attempt: Apply exact Abel summation inside each fixed Farey minor cell, separate endpoint from smooth variation loss, and test the inherited X^(-1/6) endpoint-saving candidate without retuning.
attempts.obstruction: The observed endpoint cancellation must be derived uniformly from arithmetic structure. A finite endpoint table cannot establish that theorem or exclude adversarial future scales.
attempts.next_experiment: Derive or refute a uniform one-sided cancellation estimate for the 162 Farey-cell endpoint sums using Vaughan bilinear coefficients and weighted large-sieve geometry.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified last-twin counterexample.
